source: Library of Congress, Prints & Photographs Division
license: No known restrictions on publication (public domain — pre-1924 photograph)
title: West front of Columbia Glacier, Prince William Sound, Alaska
description: 1899 Curtis photograph from the Harriman Alaska Expedition — the expedition that named College Fjord's glaciers after East Coast colleges. Shows a glacial beach with hemlock tree in foreground and the Columbia Glacier across the bay in Prince William Sound. A handwritten note on the original reads 'Washington Glacier'.
alt: Historical 1899 photograph of a glacial bay in Prince William Sound, Alaska, with a hemlock tree in the foreground and a distant tidewater glacier across the water
verifiedBySession: 2026-04-10/11 image sourcing workflow; downloaded from tile.loc.gov, visually confirmed
